events.title: Security Council LIVE: Civilians in conflict under spotlight amid rising global toll
events.description: The Security Council is meeting in New York for its annual open debate on the protection of civilians in armed conflict amid mounting concern over rising civilian casualties, attacks on humanitarian workers and widespread destruction of homes and critical infrastructure in wars around the world. The UN's top humanitarian for crisis response told ambassadors one civilian was killed every 14 minutes last year. Follow live below from our Meetings Coverage team.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Civilians in conflict under spotlight amid rising global toll

events.title: US doctor who contracted Ebola in DRC flown to Germany for treatment
events.description: Dr Peter Stafford’s wife and four children are also being monitored for symptoms amid Ebola outbreak in Congo An American doctor who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been flown to Germany for treatment, along with his wife and four children, as the World Health Organization warned of the “scale and speed” of the outbreak. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and more than 500 cases of the hemorrhagic Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. The outbreak, which has spread into urban areas, has been declared a public health emergency requiring international response. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US doctor who contracted Ebola in DRC flown to Germany for treatment

events.title: Ebola risk is high inside DR Congo but it’s no pandemic emergency: WHO
events.description: The deadly Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda does not represent a global pandemic emergency, although the risk is high at a regional and national level, the UN health agency chief said on Wednesday.

events.title: Ebola: US ban on travellers from DRC, Uganda or South Sudan ‘not the solution’
events.description: Africa CDC says restrictions could increase public health risks and highlight ‘deeper structural injustice’ in global health A US travel ban for people coming from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda or South Sudan in response to the Ebola outbreak could make the situation worse, critics have said. The outbreak was declared a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday and continues to spread, with a new case reported in the DRC’s South Kivu province , an area under the control of armed rebel groups. Continue reading...

events.title: World News in Brief: Geopolitical tensions impact global economy, Gaza aid funding shortfall, violence in South Sudan, UN condemns deadly mosque attack
events.description: The global economy is entering a more fragile period as geopolitical conflicts, rising energy costs and financial instability threaten global growth and trade.

events.title: DR Congo Ebola outbreak spreads to rebel-held South Kivu
events.description: A case of Ebola has been confirmed in Democratic Republic of Congo's South Kivu province - hundreds ​of kilometres ‌from the outbreak's epicentre - according to the rebel alliance that controls the ⁠area. It marks an expansion of an outbreak of a rare strain that was detected last week and raises further concerns about difficulties in accessing the areas hit by the epidemic, with widespread armed violence in the country's east.

events.title: Tipping point? The scramble to contain global energy crisis
events.description: It’s easy to lecture world leaders about how fuel subsidies ruin the environment and bleed state coffers dry, but try telling that to beleaguered Bolivians and cornered Kenyans… two of the many nations where the energy crisis has reached breaking point with citizens unable to keep up with prices at the pump. It’s not just in the Americas, Africa and Asia where the Iran war’s destroying livelihoods and derailing fiscal roadmaps.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Tipping point? The scramble to contain global energy crisis

events.title: Unrest in Bolivia as protests cause widespread disruption and force cabinet reshuffle
events.description: International concern has been growing following weeks of nationwide protests in Bolivia - with the country's president saying he will reshuffle his cabinet in response to the unrest.

events.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a public health emergency after 80 suspected deaths
events.description: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda an international public health emergency after dozens of suspected deaths were reported.

events.title: How the Hormuz crisis keeps disrupting kitchens, ports and paychecks
events.description: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran may have eased fears of a wider regional war, but persistent instability around the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global trade, drive up energy costs and fuel a growing jobs and cost-of-living crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How the Hormuz crisis keeps disrupting kitchens, ports and paychecks

events.title: WHO head 'deeply concerned' over 'scale and speed' of Ebola spread, says emergency committee will meet
events.description: WHO chief convenes emergency committee over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, citing over 500 suspected cases and 131 fatalities from the virus.

events.title: 30,000 people flee fresh violence in Haiti as hunger crisis deepens
events.description: Around 30,000 people have had to flee their homes in the Haitian capital Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas following a new wave of violent attacks and atrocities perpetrated by armed gangs over the past ten days.

events.title: General Assembly backs historic World Court climate crisis ruling
events.description: A landmark General Assembly resolution adopted on Wednesday is “a powerful affirmation” of international law, climate justice and science, according to UN chief António Guterres.

events.title: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime
events.description: Charges filed in Miami against 94-year-old for allegedly shooting down exiles’ planes in 1996 The United States issued a federal criminal indictment against Raúl Castro , Cuba ’s former president, and five others on Wednesday in a significant escalation of the Trump administration ’s campaign to oust the country’s six-decades-old communist regime. The 94-year-old political figurehead was charged in Miami, Florida , with conspiracy to kill US nationals, four counts of murder and two counts of destruction of aircraft. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro as it seeks to oust regime

events.title: Ebola: Experts warn of scale and spread of deadly virus
events.description: Health experts have warned the world is seeing just the "top of the iceberg" as the deadly Ebola outbreak spreads. Meanwhile, India and the African Union have postponed a scheduled summit due to the health emergency.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola: Experts warn of scale and spread of deadly virus

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Concerns grow over nuclear safety after UAE drone strike
events.description: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session in New York to discuss the situation in the Middle East following reports of a drone strike near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the United Arab Emirates. IAEA Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi briefed members as concerns grow over nuclear safety and security in the region. Follow our live coverage below for updates and for coverage of all key meetings at UN Headquarters, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Concerns grow over nuclear safety after UAE drone strike

events.title: Is the US deepening its military involvement in Nigeria?
events.description: Airstrikes in Nigeria led by the US have weakened the so-called Islamic State (IS) terror group. The joint operation shows an expanding US role, but analysts warn of limited long‑term impact and civilian risks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Is the US deepening its military involvement in Nigeria?

events.title: Papua New Guinea warns against fishing in New Ireland after mystery deaths of marine life
events.description: Initial testing found evidence of metals in water samples, months after province’s residents began reporting unusual numbers of dead fish washing ashore Papua New Guinea’s government has warned communities not to fish from parts of the New Ireland coastline as preliminary tests show evidence of metals in some water samples, after months of residents reporting dead marine life in the area. On 7 May the fisheries minister, Jelta Wong, said initial testing conducted by an independent company detected various metals in water samples taken from affected areas around Kafkaf village and Larairu lagoon in New Ireland, an island in eastern PNG. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Papua New Guinea warns against fishing in New Ireland after mystery deaths of marine life

events.title: Ebola outbreak: ‘Every epidemic begins in a community and ends in a community’
events.description: Facing a fast-moving Ebola outbreak caused by a rare strain of the virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) is relying on a rapid, community-centred response to halt transmission in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, a senior official has told UN News.

events.title: Flak jackets and final goodbyes: Lebanon’s first responders under fire
events.description: Before heading to strike sites in war-torn Lebanon, rescue workers and paramedics often say goodbye to one another – a ritual captured in widely shared videos reflecting the growing dangers faced by aid workers since hostilities between Hezbollah and Israel erupted on 2 March.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Flak jackets and final goodbyes: Lebanon’s first responders under fire

events.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
events.description: The companies were found guilty by a French court over an air disaster which killed 228 people.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash

events.title: Colombia’s climate crossroads: Trumpism casts shadow over presidential battle
events.description: Colombia is a global leader in climate activism. Could US influence drag country to a future of mining and fracking? Several hours after dark in a quiet Caribbean neighbourhood, a cluster of environmental activists gather on plastic chairs between a mango tree and a courtyard wall emblazoned with the words “Colombia, respira!” (Breathe, Colombia). So many people have turned up that some have to stand. That is because tonight’s speaker is Susana Muhamad , one of the most admired socio-environmental campaigners in the world, and this is a moment of profound historical significance. Continue reading...

events.title: Ebola patient in Berlin: Why Germany's helping
events.description: An Ebola‑infected American doctor is being treated under the highest safety standards in a specialized isolation unit in the German capital, Berlin. The facilities are considered to be among the best equipped worldwide.

events.title: Ukraine's bird lovers long for peaceful skies
events.description: As the war rages on, Ukraine's birds are increasingly becoming victims of the battlefield, pushing volunteers and soldiers to try to rescue and treat injured birds. Skies once filled with birds are now crowded with drones, and constant aerial bombardments have wrecked a delicate ecosystem across a 1,200-kilometre frontline – including birds' nesting grounds and migratory routes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine's bird lovers long for peaceful skies

events.title: Are Xi and Putin still ‘best friends’? – The Latest
events.description: Xi Jinping welcomed Vladimir Putin to Beijing with pomp and pageantry, just days after hosting Donald Trump. But as Russia’s war in Ukraine makes Moscow increasingly dependent on China, and western leaders thaw relations with Beijing, what does the power imbalance mean for Xi and Putin’s relationship? Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s deputy head of international news, Devika Bhat Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Are Xi and Putin still ‘best friends’? – The Latest

events.title: Philippine justice chief orders arrest of senator wanted by the ICC over Duterte-era killings
events.description: The Philippines justice chief has ordered the enforcement of an International Criminal Court warrant for a senator wanted on an alleged crime against humanity charge
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippine justice chief orders arrest of senator wanted by the ICC over Duterte-era killings

events.title: ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst
events.description: Human intelligence penetrated deep local networks to kill ISIS West Africa commander al-Minuki, described as the group's biggest loss in years.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst

events.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
events.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief

events.title: War is becoming a death sentence for women and girls
events.description: Thirteen-year-old Mona still remembers the moment the second airstrike hit.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War is becoming a death sentence for women and girls

events.title: Building the smart city: Promise, pitfalls and the people at its heart
events.description: From AI-powered transit systems to digital twins and flood-proof parks, cities are embracing technology at an unprecedented scale. But as innovation accelerates, experts warn that inclusion, trust and security will determine who truly benefits.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Building the smart city: Promise, pitfalls and the people at its heart

events.title: Rubio criticizes WHO’s Ebola response as US continues sweeping public health cuts
events.description: US secretary of state says WHO was ‘a little late’ in identifying deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said on Tuesday that the World Health Organization (WHO) was “a little late” in identifying the deadly Ebola outbreak in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. On Tuesday, Rubio told reporters: “The lead is obviously going to be CDC [Centers for Disease Control] and the World Health Organization, which was a little late to identify this thing unfortunately.” Continue reading...

events.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
events.description: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers

events.title: Germany reaches out to Gulf states about Iran war
events.description: Germany no longer believes it can play a mediating role in the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran. Instead, the German government is working to strengthen its ties with the Gulf states.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany reaches out to Gulf states about Iran war

events.title: Russia holds nuclear drills on land, sea and air, joined by its ally Belarus
events.description: Trucks carrying intercontinental ballistic missiles rumbled over forest roads, submarines set sail from Arctic and Pacific ports, and crews scrambled into warplanes as Russia and neighboring Belarus held the final stage of their joint nuclear drills
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia holds nuclear drills on land, sea and air, joined by its ally Belarus

events.title: This hard-line Iranian general is a major player in talks with US over war
events.description: A hard-line Iranian general linked to notorious attacks at home and abroad is believed to have seized a place near the center of power as negotiations with the United States hang in the balance
events.ticker_label: 🪖 This hard-line Iranian general is a major player in talks with US over war

events.title: Man dies after attack by 13-foot great white shark near popular island resort: officials
events.description: A 38-year-old man was killed in a shark attack near Rottnest Island off Australia's western coast, authorities say, urging additional caution in the area.

events.title: Final two bodies of missing Italians recovered from inside Maldives cave
events.description: The two were among five Italian nationals who drowned in last week's scuba diving accident in the Maldives.

events.title: Bolivia rocked by protests as US warns of ‘coup d’état’
events.description: Clashes between demonstrators and police in La Paz have entered second week, shaking centre-right president Protests blocking roads across Bolivia and turning the centre of the capital, La Paz, into a battleground between demonstrators and police have entered a second week. It is the most turbulent moment of the centre-right president Rodrigo Paz Pereira’s mere six months in office since he ended nearly two decades of rule by the leftwing Movimiento al Socialismo (Mas). Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bolivia rocked by protests as US warns of ‘coup d’état’

events.title: Teenager among 3 dead in Turkey after floods and landslides hit southern province
events.description: Officials say three people have died during flooding in southern Turkey
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Teenager among 3 dead in Turkey after floods and landslides hit southern province

events.title: World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees
events.description: The Representative of the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, in Ukraine has strongly condemned the deadly and destructive Russian missile and drone strikes in Dnipro on Tuesday night.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees

events.title: ‘I had a good life and I lost it’: How one man survived four years on the streets
events.description: A missed warning sign. A forged cheque. A passport confiscated in a foreign country. Within months, the life Latyr Thioye had built began to unravel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘I had a good life and I lost it’: How one man survived four years on the streets

events.title: Nancy Guthrie case points to criminal underworld 'wrench attack' theory, retired detective says
events.description: Expert says Nancy Guthrie's apparent abduction shows signs of a "wrench attack," where "mastermind" hackers recruit local muscle to carry out kidnappings.

events.title: Illegal immigrant truck driver from India arrested in deadly California hit-and-run after Biden release
events.description: An illegal alien truck driver was arrested after allegedly causing a fatal multi-vehicle crash near Lodi, California, federal officials said.

events.title: Two suspected American communist insurgents killed in clash in the Philippines
events.description: Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem were among 19 killed in a Philippine army firefight with suspected communist insurgents tied to the New People’s Army.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two suspected American communist insurgents killed in clash in the Philippines

events.title: Ronaldo scores twice to seal Saudi Pro League at last with Al-Nassr
events.description: Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo leads Al-Nassr to Saudi Pro League title in last game before World Cup 2026.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ronaldo scores twice to seal Saudi Pro League at last with Al-Nassr

events.title: Flotilla activists recount Israeli violence as they arrive in Turkiye
events.description: Activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla arrived in Istanbul on Thursday and described Israeli violence.

events.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
events.description: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is set to go ahead this weekend despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno

events.title: Penélope Cruz and Glenn Close star in Spanish civil war gay drama at Cannes
events.description: Penélope Cruz leads 'La Bola Negra', the new film from the creators of 'La Mesías', set partly during the Spanish Civil War and following three gay men across generations as they navigate desire, repression and inherited trauma. Glenn Close also stars in the ambitious Spanish-language drama. Meanwhile Lukas Dhont returns to Cannes after 'Close' with 'Coward', a First World War story questioning what courage really means – fighting in battle, or daring to live openly as yourself.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Penélope Cruz and Glenn Close star in Spanish civil war gay drama at Cannes

events.title: Powering the AI inference boom: Is it time to downsize the data centre?
events.description: In the world of artificial intelligence, the focus used to be on training AI models. Now, increasingly, it's on "inference" – the actual execution of the tasks we ask these systems to perform. David Gurlé is a French tech entrepreneur whose startup Antimatter aims to service the inference boom with energy solutions, mini data centres that fit inside a container and appropriate software – for only a fraction of the cost and time.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Powering the AI inference boom: Is it time to downsize the data centre?

events.title: India-Africa summit postponed as aid groups warn Ebola outbreak is ‘gaining momentum’
events.description: Healthcare workers and aid groups in eastern Congo have appealed for more supplies and staff to respond to the outbreak of a rare type of Ebola, as armed groups continued to threaten safety of the region already grappling with displacement and humanita...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India-Africa summit postponed as aid groups warn Ebola outbreak is ‘gaining momentum’

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Ambassadors debate future of Gaza amid stalled progress on lasting peace
events.description: The Security Council is discussing the stalled progress in Gaza as the fragile ceasefire there limps on, and deteriorating conditions across the West Bank amid continued civilian casualties and mounting humanitarian needs. Discussions are likely to focus on who governs Gaza, and how - with no disarmament in sight, together with efforts to support recovery.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Ambassadors debate future of Gaza amid stalled progress on lasting peace

events.title: Man who murdered teen TikTok star in Pakistan gets death sentence
events.description: Sana Yousaf's murder highlights a pattern of violence against women in Pakistan, activists say.

events.title: US is ‘simply choosing not to stop’ Ebola outbreak after massive public health cuts, experts say
events.description: Hundreds of cases reported in the DRC after USAID has been dismantled and key scientific research canceled A previously undetected outbreak of Ebola is coursing through parts of central Africa, and the US appears to be doing little to help stop it, after massive cuts to global and domestic public health efforts. There is no cure and no vaccine for the rare Bundibugyo variant of Ebola, which has caused two outbreaks in recent decades. Health leaders and scientists are now racing to understand where the virus is spreading and attempting to stop it – but the US is notably absent in these efforts. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘It’s broken English’: MP’s attempt to speak Jamaican in parliament sparks language row
events.description: Parliamentary rule that only English is allowed has reignited debate about language, legitimacy and postcolonial identity When the Jamaican MP Nekeisha Burchell stood up to give her maiden speech, she was keenly aware of how much her country’s parliament mirrored the Westminster version thousands of miles away in London. As in the UK, the session on 12 May had started with the arrival of the ceremonial mace – a 1.7-metre ornamented silver staff representing the British monarch’s authority over parliament – which now rested on a table between the government and the opposition. Despite the heat outside, debate was presided over by the speaker dressed in a ceremonial robe. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘It’s broken English’: MP’s attempt to speak Jamaican in parliament sparks language row

events.title: Cuba's Raúl Castro indicted over 1996 fatal downing of two aircraft
events.description: Miami prosecutors have prepared an indictment against former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of two NGO planes that killed four people onboard.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Cuba's Raúl Castro indicted over 1996 fatal downing of two aircraft

events.title: France annouonces €710 million in new energy aid
events.description: French prime minister Sébastien Lecornu announced new support measures to cushion the impact of the energy crisis caused by the war in Iran. The €710 million package includes extending existing support schemes for sectors such as fishing, farming and construction, as well as a new bonus for taxi drivers to switch to electric cars. But the prime minister ruled out blanket subsidies like fuel tax cuts as Paris struggles to bring its debt and deficit down.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France annouonces €710 million in new energy aid

events.title: Racism fears spark city to nix gunshot detection tech days after crazed gunman opened fire in streets
events.description: Cambridge, Massachusetts, city council voted to end its ShotSpotter contract amid racism concerns despite a recent mass shooting on Memorial Drive.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Racism fears spark city to nix gunshot detection tech days after crazed gunman opened fire in streets

events.title: Bodies of four missing Italian divers found inside 'shark cave' in Maldives days after they vanished
events.description: Rescuers found four Italian divers' bodies deep inside a Maldives underwater cave days after they vanished during a dive far beyond recreational limits.

events.title: Maldives military diver dies searching for four Italian divers missing inside underwater cave system
events.description: A Maldivian military diver died from decompression sickness during a perilous mission to recover bodies of four Italian divers trapped in a deep underwater cave.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Maldives military diver dies searching for four Italian divers missing inside underwater cave system

events.title: Iran’s World Cup hopes hit US visa hurdles
events.description: Players from Iran’s national football team are in Turkey applying for US visas for the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Cubans outraged at US charges against Raúl Castro as fears of military strikes grow
events.description: It’s a nerve-racking time for Havana neighbours of top Cuban officials as fears of US attack mount A new question in being asked in Havana as people digest the news that the US has brought criminal charges against Cuba’s 94-year-old former president, Raúl Castro: who’s your neighbour? If you happen to live near a senior figure in Cuba’s government or armed forces, others suck their teeth in an expression of concerned sympathy. For the first time, US military strikes on the island are being considered a serious possibility. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cubans outraged at US charges against Raúl Castro as fears of military strikes grow

events.title: One Nation’s rapid national expansion in disarray as ‘significant risks’ force dissolution of new branches
events.description: Exclusive: Documents seen by Guardian Australia also show new branches and members will be subject to strict gag orders Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation’s rapid expansion of local branches across the country is in disarray, with the party being forced to dissolve and re-establish its new network less than eight months after the ambitious roll out began. Documents seen by Guardian Australia show the party’s new general manager, Kelvin Morton, issued a directive to the party’s branches in April ordering committee members to properly reconstitute their branches after an internal review uncovered “significant risks”. Continue reading...

events.title: Ebola treatment centre in DR Congo burnt down as anger grows
events.description: First, an ebola treatment centre in a town at the heart of the outbreak in DR Congo is torched by scared and frustrated locals in the midst of a health crisis that claimed at least 159 lives so far and continues to spread. Also, we talked with our guest about how computer science researchers and data workers from Africa are suffering some of the worst mental health effects of the industry. And, a small bakery is helping the job market rise. Khayelitsha Cookies, a women-led business on the outskirts of Cape Town, has secured a deal to supply British Airways.

events.title: Ebola treatment hospital burned down in DRC as outbreak spreads
events.description: Protesters set a hospital on fire in the Democratic of Congo, as authorities struggle to contain an Ebola outbreak.

events.title: WiseTech begins redundancies – but omits ‘AI’ from emails to Chinese employees, workers say
events.description: ASX-listed company announced in February it would lay off almost 30% of its 7,000-strong workforce across 40 countries Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates WiseTech has begun informing staff that they will lose their jobs as part of redundancies the company has said are due to artificial intelligence advancements – although an email to staff in China omitted the word “AI” after a court case against another company in the country. Staff at WiseTech have been waiting almost three months to be told if they are among the 2,000 people the logistics software company is to cut due to advances in AI. The Australian Stock Exchange-listed company announced in late February it would lay off almost 30% of its 7,000-strong workforce across 40 countries. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WiseTech begins redundancies – but omits ‘AI’ from emails to Chinese employees, workers say

events.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
events.description: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.

events.title: Saudi Arabia: 1.5 million join Hajj pilgrimage despite war
events.description: Saudi Arabia has been directly hit by missiles and drones during the Iran war, but the country is hosting the all-important Muslim pilgrimage anyway.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Saudi Arabia: 1.5 million join Hajj pilgrimage despite war

events.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump's man at the Fed or independent voice?
events.description: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kevin Warsh: Trump's man at the Fed or independent voice?

events.title: Suspected Ebola cases reach 600 and more expected - WHO
events.description: There are now 600 suspected Ebola cases after the outbreak in Congo and Uganda, the World Health Organisation has said.

events.title: US, Nigeria strike ISIS fighters again from the air after killing senior leader
events.description: U.S. and Nigerian forces struck ISIS targets in Nigeria days after President Trump announced the killing of global ISIS leader Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US, Nigeria strike ISIS fighters again from the air after killing senior leader

events.title: Board of Peace envoy warns ‘permanent’ Gaza divide under current status quo
events.description: UN Security Council urged to press for Israeli obligations and Hamas disarmament under Gaza 'ceasefire' roadmap.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Board of Peace envoy warns ‘permanent’ Gaza divide under current status quo

events.title: News live: Australian flotilla activists released by Israel arrive in Istanbul and claim they were denied food and water
events.description: Eleven Australians were among pro-Palestine activists detained by Israel from the Global Sumud flotilla. Follow today’s news live Israel’s ambassador tells ABC flotilla detainees unharmed Israel’s ambassador to Australia, Dr Hillel Newman, claimed last night no one taken into custody from humanitarian aid flotillas headed to Gaza were harmed, after a far-right Israeli minister posted a video of himself abusing bound activists captured drew widespread condemnation. No one, out of the 400 plus people that were on the flotilla, no one was harmed. The interception itself was done with great sensitivity by the state of Israel, by our security forces. There are many accusations which are thrown out there … which are untrue. Many of us haven’t eaten for days. We were denied water for two days. … But it is nothing compared to what happens to Palestinians in the occupied territories every single day. … The message to our Australian government is that they need to cut ties with Israel. We need to cancel the alliance, stop sending them weapons parts, stop giving them political support. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News live: Australian flotilla activists released by Israel arrive in Istanbul and claim they were denied food and water

events.title: Did US aid cuts worsen Ebola outbreak in Central Africa?
events.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda are fighting to contain a dramatic Ebola outbreak, which went unnoticed for a long time. Are US aid cuts to blame for the delayed crisis response?

events.title: Gunmen open fire in two separate attacks in Honduras, killing at least 16 people
events.description: Gunmen have opened fire in two separate attacks on the Honduran coast, killing at least 16 people, including six police officers

events.title: Brazilian grieving father says justice still missing after Airbus, Air France guilty verdict
events.description: A Paris appeals court has found Airbus and Air France guilty of manslaughter in the 2009 crash of Flight 447 from Rio to Paris

events.title: A displaced Lebanese grandmother feeds thousands of compatriots who fled war

events.title: UN agencies step up Ebola response in eastern DR Congo
events.description: United Nations agencies have moved swiftly to support efforts to contain the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), delivering emergency medical supplies, protective equipment and logistics support.

events.title: Virginia judge drops case against school official accused of ignoring warnings before 6-year-old shot teacher
events.description: A Virginia judge dismissed charges against Ebony Parker, saying the legislature would need to codify her alleged actions as a crime to prosecute her.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Virginia judge drops case against school official accused of ignoring warnings before 6-year-old shot teacher

events.title: Canada confirms hantavirus case linked to cruise ship outbreak that has killed three passengers
events.description: Canada confirms a hantavirus case linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak that has killed three people and sickened multiple passengers.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: MPs urged to ignore fearmongering and pass Labor’s ‘long overdue’ negative gearing and CGT changes
events.description: Exclusive: Any attempt to use the budget measures as an excuse to raise rent is opportunistic profiteering, housing advocates say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s peak community and housing groups have urged federal parliament to quickly pass Labor’s changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax, saying the reforms would improve fairness for renters and young homebuyers. The government may introduce its negative gearing and CGT changes into parliament as soon as the coming sitting fortnight, with hopes of passing the legislation soon after with the support of the Greens. Continue reading...

events.title: France condemns Israel as it deports 37 French nationals over Gaza flotilla
events.description: All 37 French nationals detained aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla to Turkey were deported by Israel on Thursday. Paris summoned a senior Israeli diplomat over a video showing the country's national security minister taunting the multinational group of activists while they were being detained.

events.title: Rowdy teen takeover hits popular beach hot spot with wave of arrests, assaults, emergency curfew: video
events.description: Six people were reportedly arrested after a teen takeover at a New Jersey shore town led to brawls, disorder and an emergency curfew in Long Branch.

events.title: Record number of climbers summit Mount Everest from Nepali side despite overcrowding concerns
events.description: A record 274 climbers summited Mount Everest in a single day this week, raising fresh concerns about dangerous overcrowding on the world's tallest peak.

events.title: Cuba is a national security threat to the US, Rubio says
events.description: Cuba's foreign minister accused Rubio of trying to "instigate a military aggression".

events.title: NYC launches lottery for $50 World Cup football tickets
events.description: New York City is launching a lottery for 1,000 discounted World Cup tickets priced at $50.

events.title: Five key takeaways from Democrats’ autopsy report on Kamala Harris’s loss
events.description: Riddled with mistakes and missing sections, 192-page document on 2024 election loss to Trump fails to mention Gaza.

events.title: Mayor cuts limited World Cup tickets for New Yorkers to $50 after furore
events.description: Pricing for 2026 World Cup has been under heavy scrutiny, including in New York where city mayor cuts limited tickets.

events.title: Macron backs symbolic repeal of France's slavery laws, warns against 'false promises' on reparations
events.description: President Emmanuel Macron endorsed repealing centuries-old royal decrees that codified slavery in French colonies on Thursday, calling their continued existence “an offence” to the Republic. Marking 25 years since France recognised slavery as a crime against humanity, Macron also reopened debate on reparations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Macron backs symbolic repeal of France's slavery laws, warns against 'false promises' on reparations

events.title: Fans create AI-generated team songs ahead of World Cup
events.description: Fan-made football anthems are raking in millions of plays on social media, rivalling FIFA commissions from musicians.

events.title: Do aid flotillas make a difference for Palestinians under Israeli blockade?
events.description: Israeli minister's mistreatment of abducted activists sparks global condemnation.

events.title: Who are the 10 best FIFA World Cup players of all time?

events.title: Shocked Maguire posts on Insta that he is not in England’s World Cup squad
events.description: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has announced on social media that he is not going to World Cup 2026.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
events.description: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

events.title: Rami Malek compares new Cannes role to Freddie Mercury performance
events.description: Oscar-winning actor Rami Malek has returned to the Cannes spotlight with "The Man I Love", the latest film from indie director Ira Sachs, premiering on the Croisette alongside co-stars Rebecca Hall, Ebon Moss-Bachrach and Tom Sturridge. The film sees Malek play a downtown New York performance artist during the late 1980s AIDS crisis – a role the actor compared to portraying Freddie Mercury in "Bohemian Rhapsody".

events.title: SpaceX unveils IPO plans that could make Musk world's first trillionaire
events.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX has publicly filed details about its upcoming initial public offering, highlighting both the company's ambitious goals in space and its financial results. A successful share sale could value the company at a record-setting $1.75 trillion, which would put its founder on track to become the first trillionaire in history.

events.title: Ebola outbreak spreading as crowd sets fire to treatment centre in DRC
events.description: Fears are growing that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is gathering momentum, as a treatment centre is set alight by an angry crowd.

events.title: Star-studded refugee football team takes the pitch for UN
events.description: A symbolic team of famous footballers with first hand experience of life as a refugee was announced on Tuesday by UNHCR, just weeks ahead of the world’s biggest sporting event - the FIFA Men’s World Cup.

events.title: Danes try to tow dead whale from island beach after failed German rescue

events.title: Iran war squeezes Europe’s economy as growth slumps, prices surge
events.description: Europe’s economy is slowing sharply as the Iran war drives up energy costs, deepening pressure on households and challenging policy makers. Fresh data for May showed euro zone business activity contracted at its fastest pace in more than two years while inflation pressures intensified, raising fears of a prolonged cost-of-living crunch and possible recession.

events.title: Mexican mother finds remains of son missing for seven years
events.description: A Mexican mother found the bones of her son in the desert, seven years after he was kidnapped by a drug cartel. The woman is a well-known figure in Mexico, where she leads a collective of mothers looking for their missing loved ones.

events.title: ‘How are we going to survive this?’ Wellington faces six-month wait to halt sewage spill
events.description: Millions of litres of sewage have been spilling into the capital city’s waters since February after the catastrophic failure of a Moa Point wastewater plant A fix to stop millions of litres of sewage continuing to pour into the waters off the coast of New Zealand’s capital, Wellington will be in place by November, officials have said, with full repairs at the cost of NZ$53.5m by late next year. More than 100 days since the catastrophic failure of the city’s wastewater treatment plant on 4 February, a mix of raw and partially screened human effluent is still being flushed directly into the Pacific Ocean. Continue reading...

events.title: Enhanced Games: Worse than the risk of 'crippling injuries' is the threat of gene editing in sports altering our fundamental human biology
events.description: Doping in sports is nothing new. Athletes at the Enhanced Games will know the risks — severe injury, even paralysis. But bioethicists are also concerned we're opening the gates to altering our fundamental human biology.

events.title: Cannes, Canal+, Bolloré, blacklists: Is France's cultural diversity under threat?
events.description: Is France's cultural diversity under threat? As the UN celebrates cultural diversity, and as the Cannes Film Festival weathers a fight over a possible blacklist at film producer Canal+, we take a look at what happened in Cannes. We dive into the concerns over the conservative billionaire Vincent Bolloré's influence in the French cultural landscape and explore whether France's diverse offering of culture is under threat.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Feral horse numbers surge in Kosciuszko national park after pause in aerial culling, survey shows
events.description: Conservationists say population rebound demands rethink of retention zones that allow thousands of the animals to remain in the park Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Feral horse numbers in the Kosciuszko national park have surged, with new survey data estimating populations climbed by thousands after the New South Wales government paused aerial culling in 2025. Conservation advocates say the rebound in numbers demands an urgent rethink of retention zones that allow thousands of horses to remain in the park. Continue reading...

events.title: From favelas to informal settlements, new UN report points to housing solutions that work
events.description: From Thailand to Jordan, from Brazil to Germany, new approaches to housing are quietly taking shape. Residents of informal settlements once facing eviction are rebuilding their communities with State support. Refugees and host communities are reclaiming neglected spaces, turning them greener and safer. In Brazil, favelas are being upgraded rather than torn down, while in Germany, rent controls are helping to steady the market.

events.title: Macron says France must address issue of reparations for its role in slave trade
events.description: French president’s use of term comes as demand grows for formal discussion on addressing legacies of enslavement Emmanuel Macron has said reparations for France’s role in hundreds of years of enslavement of African people is an issue that should be addressed, but he stopped short of making clear proposals. “How to repair … is a question that must not be refused,” the French president said in a speech on the legacies of slavery at the Élysée Palace. “It’s also a question on which we must not make false promises.” Continue reading...

events.title: Palestinians drop bid for a senior U.N. role after U.S. pressure

events.title: Latvia scrambles NATO jets in drone alert, the latest of several in Baltic
events.description: Latvia's military said NATO planes responded to at least one drone flying in its airspace. It's the latest in a string of such incidents in the Baltic, one of which eventually toppled Latvia's government.

events.title: DW Exclusive: Germany’s far-right AfD vows to 'make history'
events.description: The AfD looks set to take power in a German state for the first time. In a DW exclusive, the frontrunner in Saxony-Anhalt discusses plans to detain immigrants and revamp the school system, and relations with Russia.

events.title: Trump's popularity plummets as inflation soars
events.description: As inflation continues to rise in the United States, Donald Trump’s popularity is at an all-time low. According to a recent poll, 46 percent of Americans are unhappy with his economic policies, with consumer spending sluggish and anxiety mounting.

events.title: String of burglaries rocking LA residential area committed by South American gangs, DA says
events.description: Seven suspects face felony charges in connection with at least 20 organized residential burglaries across Los Angeles County, prosecutors announced.

events.title: American tourists arrested in Japan after alleged break-in at viral monkey Punch's enclosure

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Turkey: DW's Alican Uludag released, but trial continues
events.description: A Turkish court has allowed DW reporter Alican Uludag to leave detention as his contentious trial opens. He is accused of having "insulted" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, which in Turkey can carry a jail sentence.

events.title: Taiwan president says 'happy' to take up Trump's offer of call
events.description: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te said Thursday he would be "happy" to talk to US leader Donald Trump, a conversation that would break more than four decades of diplomatic protocol and drew pre-emptive ire from China. Trump told reporters on Wednesday that he would speak to Lai, as the White House weighs arms sales to the democratic island.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. events.title: ‘Indefensible’: alleged child abuse survivor takes on Albanese government over $2.5bn Nauru deal
events.description: Lawyers of Hazara man who was allegedly sexually abused by carer launch bid to prevent imminent deportation to Pacific island Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government’s $2.5bn deal with Nauru, under which hundreds of non-citizens will be sent to the tiny Pacific island, will face another legal challenge prompted by an alleged child abuse survivor. Legal representatives for Abdul*, a Hazara man who was re-detained in immigration detention earlier this month, have launched a bid to prevent the 29-year-old’s imminent deportation to Nauru by challenging its compatibility with Australia’s constitution. events.title: Same but different: how Xi and China welcomed Trump and Putin
events.description: Choreography of back-to-back visits appeared deliberately mirrored but China made sure the differences were noticed Days after Donald Trump was greeted in Beijing with a military band, an honour guard and dozens of youths waving American and Chinese flags, Vladimir Putin arrived in China to an almost identical spectacle . The choreography of the two welcomes appeared deliberately mirrored, designed to showcase Beijing’s ability to host leaders from Washington and Moscow with equal grandeur. Continue reading...

events.title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
events.description: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

events.title: France: Part of Eiffel Tower sold at auction for €450,000
events.description: A French collector bought a 14-step staircase section of the Eiffel Tower at an auction in Paris.

events.title: Turkish court ousts opposition leader in boost to Erdogan
events.description: The 2023 party congress where Ozgur Ozel was elected leader of Turkey's opposition CHP has been declared null and void. The ruling is a legal victory for President Erdogan, but Ozel has vowed not to give in.

events.title: Germany ready for bigger leadership role in NATO
events.description: As NATO foreign ministers gather in Sweden, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has made it clear Berlin is ready to take on a greater leadership role in the alliance.

events.title: Everest: Record 274 climbers summit from Nepal in single day
events.description: A record number of climbers have scaled Mount Everest from the Nepali side in a single day. Despite increasing congestion at the peak, Nepali authorities have sold a record number of climbing permits.

events.title: New Chinese surveillance leaves foreigners nowhere to hide
events.description: A Chinese cybersecurity expert has revealed to DW details of China's new high-tech policing. From ski resort facial recognition to seats on a train, the system can track anyone and compile a "holistic profile."

events.title: Trump to deploy 5,000 US troops to Poland after earlier plan was canceled
events.description: US President Donald Trump said on Thursday he was sending 5,000 troops to Poland, reversing an earlier decision to cancel the deployment as Washington presses Europe to shoulder more of its own defence burden.

events.title: Cannes Film Festival: Who will win the Palme d’Or?
events.description: What are festivalgoers predicting? How many films have they watched? We took to the Croisette in Cannes to ask people who they think will win this year’s Palme d’Or 👀📽️

events.title: Bangladesh: "Donald Trump" albino buffalo becomes social media star
events.description: An #albino buffalo bought to be sacrificed at #Bangladesh's Eid al-Adha has become a social media star over his style flock of yellow hair, earning him the nickname "Donald #Trump", in reference to the #US President's signature style. The buffalo was genetically-modified to be an albino.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bangladesh: "Donald Trump" albino buffalo becomes social media star

events.title: Paris: JR turns oldest bridge into 'monumental' cavern
events.description: The Pont Neuf is a 419 year-old bridge, the oldest in #Paris - and now a temporary #mountain cave. Parisian #artist #JR unveiled his new public work on May 21. It will be fully accessible for three weeks starting June 6, with visitors able to discover the work's soundtrack provided by an ex-member of #DaftPunk.

events.title: Gaza-bound flotilla movement: All you need to know
events.description: Another flotilla has attempted to breach Israel's naval blockade of Gaza.

events.title: A veteran Panama Canal engineer becomes first woman to lead the interoceanic waterway
events.description: Panamanian President José Raúl Mulino has announced Ilya Espino de Marotta as the new head of the Panama Canal, starting October 1

events.title: Nigeria's anti-drug agency shut down large meth laboratory in a raid
events.description: Nigeria's anti-drug agency has busted a major drug syndicate involving Nigerians and Mexicans

events.title: UK government to release papers on former Prince Andrew's appointment as trade envoy
events.description: The U.K. government is set to release confidential papers related to the former Prince Andrew’s appointment as trade envoy

events.title: A record 274 climbers scale Mount Everest in a single day
events.description: Officials say hundreds of climbers scaled Mount Everest in a single day, setting a new record with 274 successful ascents

events.title: Heat wave empties roads and markets in India as some farmers turn to nighttime work
events.description: A heat wave has gripped large parts of India with roads and markets emptying during afternoons

events.title: Guterres condemns Israeli move to militarise seized compound in occupied East Jerusalem
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Wednesday strongly condemned Israel’s decision to establish military facilities at a seized UNRWA compound in occupied East Jerusalem, calling the move “wholly unacceptable”.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Guterres condemns Israeli move to militarise seized compound in occupied East Jerusalem

events.title: Security Council reform ‘is absolutely essential’: Guterres
events.description: The UN Secretary-General on Wednesday underscored the need to effectively re-open the Strait of Hormuz and expand UN Security Council membership to curb the impunity of veto-wielding “superpowers”.

events.title: Husband, mother-in-law busted in woman's cold-case murder amid backyard dig: police
events.description: Kentucky estranged husband and his mother face murder charges in a cold case after Anna Lee Manning's 1994 disappearance dating back to 1992.

events.title: Employee of needle-distributing nonprofit allegedly caught selling fentanyl near LA park
events.description: A California man working for taxpayer-funded needle distribution nonprofit PATH was arrested for allegedly trying to sell fentanyl, authorities said.

events.title: Former Georgia Christian school teacher learns fate for sexually abusing 15-year-old student
events.description: Former Georgia teacher Sherri Delle Mauldin, 61, sentenced to 12 years in prison after admitting she sexually abused a 15-year-old student at her school.

events.title: Minnesota fraud mastermind gets nearly 42 years in prison in massive $250M 'Feeding Our Future' scheme
events.description: "Feeding Our Future" leader Aimee Bock has received a nearly 42-year prison sentence for her role in a $250 million COVID-19 fraud conspiracy.

events.title: Manhunt underway after woman allegedly assaulted inside Harvard dorm, suspect spotted at MIT: officials
events.description: Harvard and MIT police are searching for a man accused of assaulting a woman inside a dorm and entering another residence hall on Tuesday evening.

events.title: Brooklyn mother who drowned her three children in the ocean near Coney Island sentenced to decades in prison
events.description: Brooklyn woman Erin Merdy sentenced to decades in prison after pleading guilty to drowning her three young children near Coney Island in 2022.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei using ‘bin Laden template’ to survive, learned from Abbottabad: analyst
events.description: Analyst compares Mojtaba Khamenei's disappearance from public view to Osama bin Laden's decade of hiding, calling it a strategic milestone for the U.S.

events.title: 'Written in our DNA': Polish pilots who remember Soviet rule prepare for America's most lethal fighter jet
events.description: Poland expects its first F-35 fighter jets very soon as U.S. and Polish forces train together at a key NATO air base near the alliance's eastern flank.
